enrichment.identity_notes: Newport Officers' Club is on US Navy base — Naval Station Newport. Civilian wedding requires active/retired military affiliation OR sponsorship. Either Elizabeth or Peter (or parent) is military-connected. Military families plan weddings differently — efficient, organized, value structure and follow-through.
win_angles: GF differentiator — 'doing this right for you' food-wise is THE thing
Cocktail-hour-heavy + buffet matches Comeketo strengths perfectly
Military venue logistics as a flex — most caterers don't want to deal with base access
16-month runway = trusted advisor play, tasting May/June, contract mid-summer
$187/head comfortable upper-tier — compete on experience not price
nepq_openers: Reconnect: 'Elizabeth, sorry I missed you on break — what's the best window in next day or two for 15-min call? Want to cover GF side of menu and Officers' Club logistics so you have real read on whether we're the right fit.'
Problem: 'With Officers' Club venue, what's been hardest about finding caterers willing to work through base access pieces?'
Identity: 'You mentioned GF mattering — that tells me you've been let down before. What does doing this right for you actually look like food-wise?'
Anchor: 'At your guest count + venue + cocktail-hour-heavy vision, $X-Y range. Bigger question — total or how money splits across food/bar/service?'
open_gaps: Is Elizabeth or Peter the military-affiliated person?
Do we have base catering vendor approval?

enrichment.strategic_note: Update status from 'Probably Not' to at least 'Maybe' — she actively asked to be called. Misclassification biases cadence wrong. Reconnect ASAP, book discovery call where GF + base-logistics conversations happen, then May 17 tasting. 16-month patience deal requires showing up consistently. She's hiring us slowly — be hireable.
